data.epi_content:
"Let's meet again."

As Erica's usual emotionless voice—completely different from the passionate conversation they were having just moments ago—faded away, the distinctive explosive sound of a firearm rang out.

The bullet from the small muzzle swiftly pierced through Erica's head.

The person who had been breathing normally and speaking just moments ago rolled her eyes back and lost balance, collapsing to the ground.

Blood poured from her ears, nose, and mouth, covering her once-pale face so completely that her skin color was no longer discernible.

"No, what is this, what... Ah."

With her head blown apart, there was no way to survive.

Even if someone could somehow extract the bullet and stuff the damaged brain back into a functioning body before using healing magic, it wouldn't be the original Erica anyway.

In the end, Erica died.

Definitively, with a hole in her right temple large enough to insert an index finger.

When someone is shot and a hole is created, blood doesn't just trickle out—it spurts upward like water from a fountain.

The first to move was Vivian, who was the first to be splattered by the blood shooting high into the air, surely mixed with brain matter.

"Evaaaan!! Evan!! Quick, healing magic!!! Healing magiiiic!!!"

The floor was already soaked with blood.

Though more of it belonged to others than to Erica herself.

No one paid attention to Lydia's corpse already sprawled on the ground, except for those who had been standing beside her until moments ago, sharing in her petty power.

Vivian took off her top and wrapped it around Erica's head, desperately trying to stop the brain matter from flowing out.

She did this despite knowing full well that it couldn't contain what had already escaped and what continued to pour out.

Vivian's pure white face contorted in anguish and turned crimson.

Evan, who had been momentarily stunned by the surreal scene before him, rushed over and used recovery magic.

But when a piece of metal had already stirred through the brain and created a hole, no amount of wound-closing would bring a patient back to normal—if that were possible, no one would ever die from injuries.

Perhaps because of the loud gunshots that had rung out multiple times, a crowd had gathered at the scene.

The students, who refused to watch public executions considering them filthy, and whose only experience with fighting was carefully choreographed duels where opponents avoided injuring each other, were shocked by the horrific scene.

A hole in someone's head with strange pink matter protruding and blood streaming down—it was a terrible sight, like something only demons would enjoy watching, especially for children who had never been exposed to raw violence.

And beside this scene, a beautiful woman wailing as she tried desperately to stop the cruel wound of her friend made it even worse.

In fact, to be precise, Erica wasn't dead yet.

At least not her body, if not her mind.

While being shot in the head often results in instant death, most victims roll their eyes back and experience slight trembling in their limbs as muscles convulse before eventually going limp.

And Erica, still tightly gripping the pistol in her right hand, had fallen backward and was trembling with her left hand and legs.

At least, she was still alive.

Knowing this fact, the Crown Prince, who had been standing among the surrounding crowd, walked over to Evan.

He approached and lightly tapped Evan's shoulder. Evan looked up with a face covered in tears and saliva, and despite seeing the Crown Prince before him, he just continued crying while using healing magic.
data.epi_content2:
The Crown Prince, disregarding Evan's unsightly appearance, spoke softly.

"Don't save her. The hole is already starting to close up.

I didn't know she was such an exceptional magician."

"...What?"

The Crown Prince pushed away Evan's hands, which had been placed on the wound trying to stop the bleeding and close it.

The blue light that had been gradually filling the hole in Erica's body, returning her brain to its place, suddenly disappeared.

Then Vivian, who had been experiencing despair and frustration at her own helplessness, rose from her place and did something unimaginable for a commoner.

Yes, she dared to grab the Crown Prince by the collar.

"What on earth are you doing?!"

If anyone other than Vivian had grabbed the Crown Prince's collar, their wrist would have been severed long ago.

"Vivian, she would be better off dead. What you're trying to save isn't Erica Mecklenburg but just a piece of meat.

A piece of meat in the shape of a girl named Erica that might breathe and twitch its fingers a bit."

At the Crown Prince's words, Evan unconsciously nodded.

After all, once a round piece of metal had stirred through and destroyed the brain, merely closing the wound wouldn't restore the head to its original state.

Nevertheless, he approached Erica, who was still alive.

Looking at the pistol tightly gripped in her right hand, he whispered.

"Why...?"

Then he tried once more to use healing magic on her head where blood was pouring out madly, but his arm fell limply.

Eventually, the strength left Erica's strangely outstretched arms, and her entire body went limp.

Yet, the hand gripping the gun showed no sign of letting go.

Evan and Vivian realized as they watched.

The fact that Erica was dead.

"I even asked her to just stay quietly in her room and die, but no one ever listens to me.

Clear the students away and arrange the bodies properly."

When the Crown Prince spoke to the shadows behind him, two shadow-like figures emerged—one to remove what were once Erica and Lydia, and the other touched the ground with a finger, instantly making the blood disappear.

Watching this, Evan wiped his tearful face with his sleeve and put on that expressionless face that Erica always wore.

"Vivian, I think I've figured out something amazing."

"...What on earth are you talking about?"

"It's the same this time too. The girl who hated pain the most didn't say anything even when her foot was cut by glass, and I thought she would never apologize, but she asked me to say sorry first. She never argued but said she was tired, suddenly begged to be saved, talked about a repetitive life, returning after death, remained calm as if her family had collapsed months ago, acted unfazed by everything as if she'd already experienced it all... And she didn't hesitate to kill someone. With a pistol she'd never fired before. She said my words wouldn't be forgotten even though I never said anything, and her last words were 'let's meet again.'"

Evan started speaking clearly but gradually sped up until he was almost mumbling to himself.
data.epi_content3:
"I have no idea what you're saying..."

Vivian sighed tiredly and said she couldn't understand. Hearing this, Evan summarized his thoughts very simply.

"Erica isn't dead.

She'll probably come back perfectly fine."

At these words, Vivian's eyes widened before she sighed again.

She crouched down and spoke softly.

"You're crazy too."

Denial of reality was something Vivian had done quite often in the past.

Anyone would if they saw their father, whom they loved and respected more than anyone else, hanging from the ceiling before they even reached puberty.

"Well, falling into delusion seems better than trying to revive and cherish a corpse."

The one who answered wasn't Evan but the Crown Prince.

He took out a pipe from his breast pocket, put it in his mouth, and created a flame at his fingertip to light it.

The distinctive pungent smell of cannabis and tobacco rose amidst the bloody scent.

Vivian bit her lip after hearing the Crown Prince's words, which showed not even a speck of courtesy or consideration.

"If you feel like you're losing your mind too, please resolve it alone in your room rather than making a scene like this friend here."

As the children had mostly dispersed, the Crown Prince was about to leave when Evan stopped him.

"Your Highness, may I open her head?"

It was clear whose head he was talking about.

With a small hope, or perhaps a twisted desire, that he might luckily find fragments in her brain—even if it was mush—Evan asked with a trembling voice, grasping at straws.

Vivian tried to interject in their conversation by crying, shouting, and expressing anger, but a woman's anger was only useful in times of peace and when situations were normal.

The Crown Prince raised the corner of his mouth, pretended to consider, and then spoke.

"Desecrating a corpse is usually considered an insult to the deceased."

"Erica is alive."

"Is that so?"

"Yes."

"I'll send someone to call for you later."

Hearing this, Vivian, still covered in Erica's blood, tried to lunge at the Crown Prince, spewing curses and royal insults that could get her beheaded, but the shadows caught her and had no intention of letting go.

Eventually, she collapsed to the ground.

She was utterly powerless.

"You're all insane."
